Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Revenue grew 17 percent year-over-year supported by enterprise demand for our cloud platform and continued adoption of productivity offerings across commercial and consumer segments.

Operating expenses rose substantially as we accelerated artificial intelligence infrastructure investment and increased data center capacity to meet growing demand ahead of planned product launches.

Free cash flow declined sequentially due to elevated capital expenditures for data center construction and networking equipment required to expand our global infrastructure footprint.

Copilot adoption across Microsoft 365 and GitHub accelerated meaningfully in the quarter, with enterprise seat counts increasing faster than our initial forecast for artificial intelligence productivity tools.

Our Intelligent Cloud segment delivered double-digit revenue growth driven by Azure and server products, reflecting continued customer migration from on-premises workloads to cloud-native architectures.

Item 3. Other Information
